title: DARPA to explore ways to rapidly rebuild satellite networks if attacked
summary: DARPA is seeking innovative solutions to quickly restore satellite networks in the event of an attack, aiming to resume critical services within hours or weeks. The initiative focuses on developing novel concepts for rapid network recovery.

title: What’s the Best Way to Monetize Space Energy?
summary: The article discusses monetizing space-based energy, highlighting how space infrastructure could become valuable for energy production. Overview Energy, led by CEO Marc Berte, explores the potential of space energy as a viable commercial opportunity.

title: Sustained maneuver has a propulsion problem
summary: A space mission faces propulsion issues during a sustained maneuver, highlighting the evolving challenges of spacecraft positioning and control beyond simple placement. The incident underscores the growing complexity of modern space architecture.

title: Missile production push runs into solid rocket motor bottleneck
summary: A CSIS report highlights a solid rocket motor supply chain bottleneck that could impact planned 2027 interceptor purchases, as the industry recovers from years of consolidation. The issue threatens missile production goals despite increased demand.

title: Revised Artemis lunar lander plans take shape
summary: NASA has outlined updated strategies for Blue Origin and SpaceX to expedite the development of Artemis lunar landers. The revisions aim to accelerate progress toward the program's goals.

title: SpaceX shares rise nearly 20% in historic IPO
summary: SpaceX shares surged nearly 20% on their first day of trading following the company's historic IPO, marking a significant milestone for both SpaceX and the space industry.

title: Avanti trims GEO exposure with Hylas-3 sale
summary: Avanti Communications is selling its Hylas-3 satellite to reduce its geostationary orbit exposure. This move marks a shift away from its previous debt-fueled expansion strategy.

title: Astronomers fear orbital data centers will interfere with observations
summary: Astronomers warn that SpaceX's planned orbital data center satellites, launching as early as next year, could severely interfere with astronomical observations. The concern highlights growing tensions between commercial space activities and scientific research.

title: All in on AI at Astra
summary: Astra's CEO Chris Kemp suggests he might be the last employee due to the company's focus on AI, following its involvement in the SPAC boom. The company is shifting its strategy to prioritize artificial intelligence over traditional space operations.

title: H3 successfully returns to flight
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight on June 11, placing six small satellites into orbit. This marks its first mission since a failure in December.

title: 'Everybody came with their A-game': Oscar-winning sound editor Andy Nelson talks reuniting with Spielberg & John Williams for 'Disclosure Day'
summary: Oscar-winning sound editor Andy Nelson discusses reuniting with director Steven Spielberg and composer John Williams for the film 'Disclosure Day'. Nelson highlights the high level of dedication and effort from the team.

title: NASA X-ray spacecraft discovers supernova wreckage at the heart of the Milky Way
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray spacecraft discovered the supernova wreckage of a dead star that exploded 1,700 years ago, ejecting debris at 2 million miles per hour. The remnants are located at the heart of the Milky Way.

title: Live from the moon! How lasers connected us all to NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts on their epic lunar trip
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission used a laser system, tested on the ISS, to stream video directly from the spacecraft during its lunar flyby. This technology enabled live communication from the moon, showcasing advancements in space data transmission.

title: Hubble spies a swarm of stars in a strange, irregular galaxy | Space photo of the day for June 15, 2026
summary: Hubble Space Telescope captures an image of a swarm of stars in an irregular galaxy. The galaxy's faint and unusual structure makes it a captivating subject for astronomers.

title: Scientists find strange changes on sun hours before a powerful X9 solar flare: 'I was not expecting what I found'
summary: Scientists observed unusual changes on the sun hours before an X9 solar flare, which could improve space weather forecasting. The unexpected findings highlight the need for better monitoring of solar activity.

title: Our experts recommend these image-stabilized Canon binoculars for stunning, steady views of the night sky, now $100 off in this early anti-Prime Day deal
summary: Canon's image-stabilized binoculars are recommended for stargazing, currently discounted by $100 in an early anti-Prime Day deal. These 18x50 IS binoculars provide shake-free, high-magnification views of celestial events like June’s moon and Mercury conjunction.

title: Catch Mercury shining at its best tonight before it slips back into the sun's glare
summary: Mercury will be at its farthest point from the sun in its current evening apparition on June 15, making it visible tonight. This event provides a rare opportunity to observe the planet before it becomes obscured by the sun's glare.

title: How Jules Verne predicted the Artemis 2 mission to the moon almost 160 years ago
summary: Jules Verne's 19th-century novels 'From the Earth to the Moon' and 'All Around the Moon' eerily predicted key aspects of NASA's Artemis 2 mission, including a conical capsule and a free-return lunar trajectory. The parallels between Verne's speculative fiction and modern spaceflight highlight the enduring influence of early science fiction on space exploration.

title: Frontiers Forum Speaker Series
summary: NASA is hosting a public speaker series featuring agency experts discussing missions, aerospace technology, science, and innovation. The series aims to engage the public and cover topics like the search for extraterrestrial life.

title: NASA Astronaut Anil Menon Available for Prelaunch Virtual Interviews
summary: NASA astronaut Anil Menon will be available for virtual prelaunch interviews on June 22 to discuss his upcoming mission to the International Space Station as part of Expeditions 74/75. The interviews will be conducted from the Gagarin Cosmonaut Training Center in Star City, Russia.

title: San Francisco’s Patchwork Streets
summary: An astronaut aboard the International Space Station captured a photograph of downtown San Francisco and nearby areas on May 27, 2026, highlighting the Golden Gate Bridge and the San Francisco-Oakland Bay Bridge.

title: Experience the Launch of NASA’s Roman Space Telescope
summary: NASA invites digital creators and social media users to register for a NASA Social event to experience the launch of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, which aims to reveal distant worlds, dark energy, and the structure of the cosmos.

title: Metrics
summary: NASA has released the FY26 Services Catalog and Service Level Agreement (SLA) for NSSC services, detailing service descriptions, rates, and service level indicators.

title: Pumice Rafts Encroach on Admiralty Islands
summary: Buoyant volcanic pumice rafts from an underwater eruption drifted across the Bismarck Sea, encroaching on the coasts of the Admiralty Islands. The phenomenon is causing ecological and navigational concerns in the region.

title: NASA to Cover 34th SpaceX Resupply Mission Space Station Departure
summary: NASA will cover the departure of SpaceX's Dragon spacecraft from the International Space Station on June 16, returning scientific samples and hardware to Earth. Live coverage will be available on NASA+, Amazon Prime, and NASA's YouTube channel starting at 11:45 a.m. EDT.

title: Black Eye Galaxy
summary: NASA's Hubble and James Webb Space Telescopes captured a composite image of Messier 64, the Black Eye Galaxy, using different wavelengths. The galaxy is characterized by its distinctive dark dust lane and bright central region.

title: Hubble Sees Swarm of Galaxies
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the galaxy cluster MACS0329-0211, resembling a swarm of bees returning to their hive. This observation contributes to the study of galaxy clusters and their gravitational effects.

title: World Cup Fever in Guadalajara
summary: Guadalajara's metro area has expanded westward since hosting World Cup matches in 1986, shaped by ancient volcanoes. The city is experiencing World Cup fever as it prepares for matches.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. The focus has shifted following the completion of Flight 12.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, marking a significant milestone in its space program. LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. The mission marks a significant milestone for the country's space program after previous setbacks.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification structural verification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This milestone marks progress in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission to test lunar landers in low Earth orbit (LEO). These veteran astronauts will prepare for future lunar landings as part of the Artemis program.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: Booster 20 has entered cryogenic testing at SpaceX's Starbase, preparing for Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: Launch Preview: Chinese, Rocket Lab, and Starlink flights scheduled
summary: The upcoming week includes space launches from China, Rocket Lab, and multiple Starlink missions. Flights are scheduled from California, Virginia, and Florida.

title: NASA concludes MAVEN mission at Mars, uses data to discover new atmospheric phenomenon
summary: NASA has concluded the MAVEN mission at Mars after a six-month effort to regain communication. The mission's data has led to the discovery of a new atmospheric phenomenon.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its Blue Moon lunar lander and Mars missions despite the recent New Glenn rocket anomaly during hotfire testing at Launch Complex 36. The company is focusing on recovering from the setback while advancing its long-term space exploration goals.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress in developing the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This advancement is a key milestone for the company's spacecraft development efforts.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on space industry developments without specifying the exact news content.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses. This marks the resumption of routine science operations for the mission.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports European space science activities by involving research institutes.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency test and develop materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space. Their work supports the success of ESA's missions by ensuring spacecraft components are space-proofed.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance both technical and educational aspects of space missions.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' suggests a focus on robust or resilient technology, likely in the space industry. The accompanying image description is minimal and lacks context.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry event involving a parachute test, likely for a Mars or planetary mission, is described as a 'great parachute bake-out'. The term suggests a thermal or stress test of the parachute system.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars landing by baking it in a sterilization oven to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 mission to search for signs of life on Mars while adhering to strict planetary protection protocols.

title: Space Stocks Dip After SpaceX IPO
summary: SpaceX's successful IPO led to a dip in stocks for other publicly traded space companies, causing major players to decline across the board.

title: JAXA Nails H3 Return to Flight
summary: JAXA successfully launched the H3 rocket on its eighth mission, marking its first successful flight with a new three-engine variant. The rocket delivered all six of its payloads to space from Japan’s Tanegashima Space Center.

title: VCs Predict the SpaceX IPO Will Lift the Entire Industry
summary: Venture capitalists anticipate that SpaceX's potential IPO will positively impact the broader space industry by attracting more investment. The move could shift reliance from private capital to public markets, benefiting other space companies.

title: Op-ed: Up to a Trillion Dollars is About to Chase Space. What Kind of Economy Will Be Built?
summary: A trillion dollars is expected to flow into the space industry, raising questions about how this capital will shape the future space economy. The largest IPO in history could drive record investment, but its impact remains uncertain.

title: What the SpaceX IPO Means for Europe’s Space Industry
summary: SpaceX's IPO in New York is a landmark event for the US space industry, but it also has significant implications for Europe's space sector. The move could reshape global competition and investment dynamics in space.

title: Exclusive: Tilebox Launches AI-Focused Update
summary: Tilebox launched an update to enhance AI agents' capabilities in analyzing geospatial data. The update aims to improve the effectiveness of AI-driven geospatial analytics.

title: Analysts Debate SpaceX’s $1.75T Valuation Ahead of IPO
summary: Analysts are divided on the accuracy of SpaceX's $1.75 trillion valuation ahead of its potential IPO. The debate highlights the booming growth of the space industry despite uncertainty about short-term stock performance.

title: Symphony Space Unveils Adagio-XL ODC Sat
summary: Symphony Space has introduced the Adagio-XL ODC satellite, marking its entry into the orbital data center market. This initiative highlights the company's expansion into space-based data storage and processing solutions.

title: Orbital Raises $5M Pre-Seed to Build ODC Constellation
summary: Orbital secures $5 million in pre-seed funding to develop its ODC constellation, which uses smaller satellites to handle large AI inference workloads and maintain constant ground communication.

title: FCC Gives Amazon Leo More Time to Launch
summary: The FCC has extended the deadline for Amazon's Project Kuiper to deploy at least half of its satellite constellation, while imposing conditions to expedite launches. The decision aims to balance regulatory flexibility with encouraging timely deployment.

title: SpaceX Begins Trading at $150 Making Elon Musk the World’s First Trillionaire on Paper
summary: SpaceX started public trading on Nasdaq at $150 per share, an 11% increase from its IPO price of $135, making Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire on paper.

title: Satellite IoT is Becoming Essential Infrastructure as NTN and Cellular IoT Services Converge
summary: Satellite IoT is emerging as critical infrastructure as Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN) and cellular IoT services integrate. Enterprises are increasingly relying on these technologies for fleet management, utilities, and remote asset monitoring.

title: U.S. State Department Taps SpaceX’s Starlink for Two-Year Disaster Response Initiative
summary: The U.S. Department of State signed a two-year agreement with SpaceX to use Starlink for global disaster response and humanitarian aid. This initiative aims to improve connectivity during emergencies.

title: Canadian Government Awards EO Satellite Contracts to Calian, Kepler, MDA Space
summary: The Canadian government awarded contracts worth C$2.4 million to Calian, Kepler, and MDA Space to enhance Earth Observation (EO) satellite capabilities.

title: Viasat Secures PTS-G U.S. Space Force SSC Swarm 1 Contract
summary: Viasat has secured a contract from the U.S. Space Force’s Space Systems Command to build, launch, and deliver the first satellite in a proliferated fleet of small satellites. This marks a significant step in the company's expansion within the U.S. defense space sector.

title: SpaceX Set for IPO, Banking on Faith in Elon Musk and Massive AI Growth
summary: SpaceX is preparing for its IPO on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol SPCX, leveraging Elon Musk's reputation and anticipated AI growth. The company, which has operated privately for 24 years, aims to go public amid high expectations.

title: Applied Atomics Emerges from Stealth with Enough Backing to Build Star Reacher Network
summary: Applied Atomics has come out of stealth mode with over $500 million in demand commitments to develop the Star Reacher network, a mobility infrastructure layer. The company aims to build a network to support space-based transportation and logistics.

title: Space Forge Secures £10 Million of New Investment through European, UK Space Agencies
summary: Space Forge has secured £10 million ($13.4 million) in new investment from the European Space Agency and UK Space Agency. The funding will support the company's space manufacturing technology.

title: Lithuania and Luxembourg Strengthen Space Defense Ties
summary: Lithuania and Luxembourg are strengthening their space defense cooperation following a meeting between their respective defense ministers. The collaboration aims to enhance joint efforts in space security and defense.

title: Another Earth Expands Into Brazil
summary: Another Earth, an Earth Observation (EO) data company, is expanding into Brazil, citing the country's climate pressures and rapid growth. The expansion aims to leverage Brazil's large market in Latin America.
